title: A tree near my home
summary: Describe a tree near your home and what it means to you.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a tree near your home. Say where it is, what it looks like, what happens around it, and how you feel about it. Use present simple.
hintStarters: Near my flat in Hanoi, there is…
The tree is very old and…
Many people sit under it because…
sampleText: Near my flat in Hanoi, there is a very big tree on the corner of a small street. I do not know the name of the tree, but the leaves are dark green and the branches are wide. The tree is much older than the buildings around it. Many people sit under the tree every day. An old woman sells hot tea on the pavement. She has four small plastic chairs and a low table. Workers stop there in the morning for tea and a cigarette. Old men play chess in the afternoon. Children sometimes climb on the lowest branch and laugh. In summer, the tree gives a lot of shade. In autumn, the yellow leaves cover the pavement. I walk past the tree every day. I always feel calm when I see it. The tree does not move, but it is full of life. I think the tree is the heart of my street. If the tree was not there, the street would feel empty and very loud.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: sells hot tea on the pavement
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bán trà nóng trên vỉa hè
lá vàng phủ kín vỉa hè

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'There is / there are' for description
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'there is' với danh từ số ít: 'there is a very big tree'. Dùng 'there are' với danh từ số nhiều: 'there are four chairs'. Cấu trúc này dùng để mô tả những gì tồn tại trong một địa điểm.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'there is' with singular nouns: 'there is a very big tree'. Use 'there are' with plural nouns: 'there are four chairs'. This structure describes what exists in a place.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adjectives before nouns
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong tiếng Anh, tính từ đứng TRƯỚC danh từ: 'a big tree', 'dark green leaves', 'a small street'. Không nói 'a tree big'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: In English, adjectives go BEFORE the noun: 'a big tree', 'dark green leaves', 'a small street'. Never say 'a tree big'.

sampleTranslationVi: Gần căn hộ của tôi ở Hà Nội, có một cây rất lớn ở góc một con phố nhỏ. Tôi không biết tên cây, nhưng lá cây màu xanh đậm và cành cây xòe rộng. Cây này già hơn nhiều so với các tòa nhà xung quanh. Nhiều người ngồi dưới cây mỗi ngày. Một bà cụ bán trà nóng trên vỉa hè. Bà có bốn cái ghế nhựa nhỏ và một cái bàn thấp. Buổi sáng, công nhân dừng lại uống trà và hút thuốc. Buổi chiều, các cụ ông chơi cờ. Đôi khi trẻ con trèo lên cành thấp nhất và cười. Mùa hè, cây cho rất nhiều bóng mát. Mùa thu, lá vàng phủ kín vỉa hè. Tôi đi ngang qua cây mỗi ngày. Tôi luôn cảm thấy bình yên khi nhìn thấy nó. Cây không di chuyển, nhưng nó đầy sự sống. Tôi nghĩ cây là trái tim của con phố. Nếu không có cây, con phố sẽ cảm thấy trống vắng và rất ồn ào.

options: On the corner of a small street near the writer's flat
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'there is a very big tree on the corner of a small street'.
The text says 'the leaves are dark green'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the old woman sell?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'An old woman sells hot tea on the pavement'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Who plays chess in the afternoon?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Old men play chess in the afternoon'.

options: The tea seller leaves the city.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'In autumn, the yellow leaves cover the pavement'.

options: There is a big tree on my street.
There are a big tree on my street.
There is many trees on my street.
There a big tree on my street.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'there is' is used with a singular noun: 'a big tree'.

options: There are a chair under the tree.
There are four chairs under the tree.
There is four chairs under the tree.
There are chair four under tree.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'there are' is used with plural nouns: 'four chairs'.
mcQuestions.hint: Match 'there are' with more than one thing.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which order is correct in English?
mcQuestions.explanation: Order: article + adjective + noun → 'a big tree'.
mcQuestions.hint: Adjective goes before the noun in English.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why does the writer call the tree 'the heart of my street'?
options: Because it has a red shape.
Because it is the centre of life on the street.
Because it is small and quiet.
mcQuestions.explanation: Many people gather under the tree all day, so it acts as the centre of street life.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about what happens around the tree.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present simple for a habit?
options: I walked past the tree yesterday.
I am walking past the tree now.
I walk past the tree every day.
I will walk past the tree tomorrow.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple with 'every day' shows a habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a time phrase that means 'always'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do trees in a city change the way people use the street around them?
